title: Hydrocarbon oxidation by water oxidation electrocatalysts in non-aqueous solvents
abstract: Provided herein are processes and systems for oxidation of a hydrocarbon reactant to generate an oxidized hydrocarbon product; said process comprising: contacting a water oxidation electrocatalyst with said hydrocarbon reactant and water in the presence of a non-aqueous solvent; wherein an anodic bias is applied to said water oxidation electrocatalyst, thereby generating said oxidized hydrocarbon product; and wherein said water oxidation electrocatalyst comprises one or more transition metals other than Ru. Optionally, said water is provided in said non-aqueous solvent at a concentration less than or equal to 0.5 vol.%. Optionally, the magnitude of said anodic bias is selected to generate said oxidized hydrocarbon product characterized by selected product distribution.
claims:
Claims We claim: 1 . A process for oxidation of a hydrocarbon reactant to generate an oxidized hydrocarbon product; said process comprising: contacting a water oxidation electrocatalyst with said hydrocarbon reactant and water in the presence of a non-aqueous solvent; wherein an anodic bias is applied to said water oxidation electrocatalyst, thereby generating said oxidized hydrocarbon product; and wherein said water oxidation electrocatalyst comprises one or more transition metals other than Ru. 2. A process for oxidation of a hydrocarbon reactant to generate an oxidized hydrocarbon product; said process comprising: contacting a water oxidation electrocatalyst with said hydrocarbon reactant and water in the presence of a non-aqueous solvent; wherein an anodic bias is applied to said water oxidation electrocatalyst, thereby generating said oxidized hydrocarbon product; wherein said water is provided in said non-aqueous solvent at a concentration less than or equal to 0.5 vol.%. 3. A process for oxidation of a hydrocarbon reactant to generate an oxidized hydrocarbon product characterized by a selected product distribution; said process comprising: contacting a water oxidation electrocatalyst with said hydrocarbon reactant and water in the presence of a non-aqueous solvent; and applying an anodic bias to said water oxidation electrocatalyst, wherein the magnitude of said anodic bias is selected to generate said oxidized hydrocarbon product characterized by said selected product distribution. 4. The process of any of claims 1 - 3, wherein said water oxidation electrocatalyst does not comprise Ru. 5. The process of any of claims 1 - 4, wherein said water oxidation electrocatalyst comprises an inorganic catalyst. 6. The process of any of claims 1 - 5, wherein said water oxidation electrocatalyst is a metal oxide or a metal hydroxide. 7. The process of claim 6, wherein said metal oxide or metal hydroxide comprises one or more earth abundant metals. 8. The process of claim 7, wherein said metal oxide or metal hydroxide comprises one or more metals selected from the group consisting of Ni, Fe, Co, Mn, Zn, Sc, V, Cr, Cu, Ti, or a lanthanide. 9. The process of any of claims 1 - 8, wherein said water oxidation electrocatalyst is a layered ionic solid. 10. The process of claim 9, wherein said layered ionic solid is a layered double hydroxide solid. 1 1 . The process of claim 10, wherein said layered double hydroxide solid comprises a Ni hydroxide, an Fe hydroxide, or a Ni-Fe hydroxide. 12. The process of any of claims 10 or 1 1 , wherein said layered double hydroxide solid is nanostructured. 13. The process of any of claims 10 - 12, wherein said layered double hydroxide solid is generated via pulse laser ablation in liquid. 14. The process of any of claims 1 - 13, wherein said water oxidation electrocatalyst is not an organometallic catalyst. 15. The process of any of claims 1 - 14, wherein said water oxidation electrocatalyst is a heterogeneous catalyst. 16. The process of any of claims 1 - 8, wherein said water oxidation electrocatalyst is a perovskite, a polyoxometalate, or a metal-organic framework. 17. The process of any of claims 1 - 16, wherein said water oxidation electrocatalyst is provided on an electrode at a loading density of 1 Î¼g/cm 2 to 1 g/cm 2 . 18. The process of any of claims 1 - 17, wherein said water oxidation electrocatalyst is provided in the form of nanoparticles. 19. The process of claim 18, wherein said nanoparticles have an average diameter selected from the range of 1 nm to 1 Î¼Î¹ÏÎ¹. 20. The process of any of claims 1 - 19, wherein said non-aqueous solvent is provided in liquid phase at a temperature selected from the range of -78 Â°C to 100 Â°C. 21 . The process of any of claims 1 - 20, wherein said non-aqueous solvent is a liquid at normal temperature and pressure (NTP). 22. The process of any of claims 1 - 21 , wherein said hydrocarbon reactant comprises a substituted or unsubstituted: C1 -C10 alkyl, C3-C10 cycloalkyl, C5- C10 aryl, C5-C10 heteroaryl, C1-C10 acyl, C1-C10 hydroxyl, C1-C10 alkoxy, C2-C10 alkenyl, C 2 -Ci 0 alkynyl, C5-C10 alkylaryl, C3-C10 arylene, C3-C10 heteroarylene, C2-C10 alkenylene, C3-C10 cylcoalkenylene, C2-C10 alkynylene, ammonium ion, or any combination thereof. 23. The process of any of claims 1 - 22, wherein said hydrocarbon reactant is selected from the group consisting of toluene, cyclohexane, cyclohexene, diphenylmethane, (2-chloroethyl)benzene, styrene, 9,1 0-dihydroanthracene, m-toluidine, methyl 5-methoxy-salicylate, 2-methylpentane, cyclohexanol, pentachlorobiphenyl, PCB 101 , tetramethylammonium hexafluorophosphate, and any combination thereof. 24. The process of any of claims 1 - 23, wherein said oxidized hydrocarbon product comprises an alcohol, an ether, an epoxide, a ketone, a carboxylic acid, an aldehyde, an acid chloride, an organic acid anhydride, or a combination thereof. 25. The process of any of claims 1 - 24, wherein said hydrocarbon reactant is provided in said non-aqueous solvent at a concentration selected from the range of 0.5 mM to 0.5 M. 26. The process of any of claims 1 or 3, wherein said water is provided in said non-aqueous solvent at a concentration less than or equal to 1 vol.%. 27. The process of any of claims 1 or 3, wherein said water is provided in said non-aqueous solvent at a concentration selected from the range of 0.1 vol.% to 5 vol.%. 28. The process of any of claims 1 - 27, wherein said water is characterized by a pH that is greater than 7. 29. The process of any of claims 1 - 28, wherein said non-aqueous solvent is a polar non-aqueous solvent. 30. The process of any of claims 1 - 29, wherein said non-aqueous solvent is a polar aprotic solvent. 31 . The process of any of claims 1 - 30, wherein said non-aqueous solvent is oxidatively stable under an applied voltage greater than 1 .5 V vs. normal hydrogen electrode (NHE). 32. The process of any of claims 1 - 31 , wherein said non-aqueous solvent is selected from the group consisting of acetonitrile, nitromethane, dichloromethane, propylene carbonate, liquid-S0 2 ,dimethyl formamide, ionic liquid, perfluorinated liquid, and any combinations thereof. 33. The process of any of claims 1 - 32, wherein said contacting step is carried out in the presence of a supporting electrolyte that is provided in said nonaqueous solvent. 34. The process of claim 33, wherein said supporting electrolyte is oxidatively stable under an applied voltage greater than 1 .5 V vs. normal hydrogen electrode (NHE). 35. The process of any of claims 33 or 34, wherein said supporting electrolyte is selected from the group consisting of a periodate salt, a perchlorate salt, a tetraalkylammonium salt, a hexafluorophosphate salt, and any combinations thereof. 36. The process of any of claims 33 - 35, wherein said supporting electrolyte is provided at a concentration selected from the range of 10 mM to 100 mM. 37. The process of any of claims 1 - 36, wherein said anodic bias is greater than or equal 0.5 V vs. normal hydrogen electrode (NHE). 38. The process of any of claims 1 - 37, wherein said anodic bias is selected from the range of 0.5 V to 5 V vs. normal hydrogen electrode (NHE). 39. The process of any of claims 1 - 38, wherein said water oxidation electrocatalyst is immobilized on an anode. 40. The process of claim 39, wherein said anode comprises fluorine-doped tin oxide (FTO), indium tin oxide (ITO), an allotrope of carbon, a metal, or any combination thereof. 41 . The process of any of claims 1 - 40, wherein a cathode is provided in contact with said non-aqueous solvent. 42. The process of claim 41 , wherein said cathode comprises platinum, nickel, carbon, titanium, or any a combination thereof. 43. The process of any of claims 1 - 42, wherein said hydrocarbon reactant comprises a C-H bond, wherein said C-H bond is oxidized to a C-0 bond or a C=0. 44. The process of any of claims 1 - 43, wherein said non-aqueous solvent has a dielectric constant greater than 10. 45. The process of any claims 1 - 44, wherein said non-aqueous solvent has dipole moment greater than 1 .5 debye. 

 The process of claim 22, wherein said hydrocarbon reactant comprises a phosphate ion, a hexafluorophosphate ion, an amine, an imine, a carbonyl, an ether, a nitrile, or a combination of these functional groups. The process of any of claims 3 - 46, wherein said anodic bias is applied for a reaction time selected to generate said oxidized hydrocarbon product characterized by said selected product distribution. A flow-through system for oxidation of a hydrocarbon reactant to generate an oxidized hydrocarbon product, said system comprising: a non-aqueous solvent; said hydrocarbon reactant provided in said non-aqueous solvent; water provided in said non-aqueous solvent; a working electrode at least partially provided in a non-aqueous solvent; a water oxidation electrocatalyst immobilized on said working electrode and in contact with said oxidized hydrocarbon product and said water; and a counter electrode at least partially provided in said non-aqueous solvent and electrically connected to said working electrode; wherein: an anodic bias is applied to said working electrode, thereby generating said oxidized hydrocarbon product; said non-aqueous solvent continuously flows through said system; and wherein said water oxidation electrocatalyst comprises one or more transition metals other than Ru. 49. The system of claim 48, further comprising a reference electrode. 50. The system of any of claims 48 or 49, wherein said water oxidation electrocatalyst does not comprise Ru.
